cb_step_5.title: Set threshold range
cb_step_5.instruction: Adjust the range to isolate the skull.

cb_step_7.title: Select skull mask segment
cb_step_7.instruction: Pick the segment you just created.

cb_step_10.title: Adjust ROI boundaries
cb_step_10.instruction: Drag handles to enclose the skull portion.

cb_step_12.title: Adjust 3D view
cb_step_12.instruction: Rotate and zoom to get a good angle for placing the cutting curve.

cb_step_14.title: Draw cutting curve
cb_step_14.instruction: Place points along the skull boundary to enclose the defect.

cb_step_15.title: Select the curve
cb_step_15.instruction: Pick the curve you just drew.

operation_descriptions: Create a new segmentation node named Cranial_Segmentation
Add a segment named Cranial_Segment to the segmentation
Open the Threshold effect in Segment Editor
Apply the threshold effect
Create a new closed curve markups node via Markups module
Hide the curve node by making it invisible
Create a new segmentation node named Cranial_Segmentation
Add a segment named Cranial_Segment to the segmentation
Open the Threshold effect in Segment Editor
Apply the threshold effect
Load the selected skull mask via Load Skull Mask button
Add an ROI node using Add ROI button
Manually adjust the boundaries of the ROI to retain the skull portion.
Crop skull mask to the ROI using Crop to ROI button
Manually adjust the 3D view to get the best angle for placing the cutting curve.
Create a new closed curve markups node via Markups module
Manually draw the curve on the skull model to enclose the fractured skull portion.
Manually draw the curve on the skull model to enclose the fractured skull portion.
Cut defect from skull using the curve via Cut Defect button
Hide the curve node by making it invisible
Generate implant model from defect via Generate Implant button

ground_api_evidence_note: ground_api_evidence is pre-revision retrieval evidence; final_api_evidence reflects the current validated templates.

failures.error: QtPropertyCalledAsMethod: 'slicer.modules.segmenteditor.widgetRepresentation().self().editor.activeEffect().setParameter' resolves to a non-callable NoneType — it is a property; read it without parentheses or use the equivalent MRML-node getter
